task_completion.description: Draw the bottom chord with 4 horizontal segments.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A horizontal line at the bottom of the structure consisting of 4 distinct segments (nodes/points visible between the segments).

task_completion.description: Draw the top chord with 3 horizontal segments.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A horizontal line at the top of the structure consisting of 3 distinct segments, positioned above the bottom chord.

task_completion.description: Draw 8 diagonal segments forming a zig-zag pattern.
task_completion.visual_evidence: 8 diagonal lines connecting the nodes of the top and bottom chords in a continuous zig-zag (W-shape/triangles) pattern.

task_completion.description: Constrain the left-most node of the bottom chord to the origin.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The leftmost point of the bottom chord is locked exactly to the central origin point (the intersection of the thick red and green axes in SolveSpace).

task_completion.description: Apply equal length constraints to all 15 line segments.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The truss forms perfect equilateral triangles, and/or equal length constraint indicators (e.g., 'equal length' in the property browser/text window) are visible.

task_completion.description: Set a distance constraint of 40.0 mm on the first bottom chord segment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dimension line with the value '40.00' (or '40.0') is attached to the first (leftmost) segment of the bottom chord.

task_completion.description: Save the resulting file to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SolveSpace window title bar displays 'warren_truss.slvs' or a save dialog shows the file being saved to '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/warren_truss.slvs'.

integrity.description: The agent used the SolveSpace GUI to create the model.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the SolveSpace graphical interface, canvas, and toolbars being used, rather than a text editor or terminal writing the .slvs file directly.

integrity.description: The agent used a purely parametric design with only one distance constraint.
integrity.visual_evidence: Only one '40.0' dimension is visible on the canvas. The property browser does not show multiple distance constraints being used to manually size each segment.

integrity.description: The geometry is genuinely drawn and constrained, not just visually approximated.
integrity.visual_evidence: The lines are solid and properly connected at nodes (indicated by constraint squares/points in SolveSpace), and the structure is fully constrained (often indicated by green lines instead of white/unconstrained lines in SolveSpace).

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file path is exactly /home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/warren_truss.slvs. The truss must consist of exactly 15 line segments (4 bottom, 3 top, 8 diagonal). The model must be purely parametric, meaning exactly 1 distance constraint of 40.0 mm is applied to the first bottom segment, and all other segments are constrained using equal length constraints.
